#include "network_config_parser.h"

/**
 * Parse network configuration parameters and store them into a buffer
 */
int parse_network_config_params(char* buffer, int buffer_size, char** params, int param_count) {
    // Validate input parameters
    if (buffer == NULL || params == NULL || param_count < 0 || buffer_size <= 0) {
        return -1;
    }

    int processed_count = 0;
    char* current_ptr = buffer;
    int remaining_space = buffer_size;

    for (int i = 0; i < param_count; i++) {
        // Validate that params[i] is not NULL
        if (params[i] == NULL) {
            continue;
        }

        // Get the length of the current parameter string
        size_t param_len = strlen(params[i]);
        
        // Check if we have enough space for the parameter string plus null terminator
        // Note: param_len + 1 because we need space for the null terminator
        if (param_len + 1 > (size_t)remaining_space) {
            // Not enough space to store this parameter
            break;
        }

        // Copy the parameter content into the buffer
        memcpy(current_ptr, params[i], param_len);
        
        // Add null terminator
        current_ptr[param_len] = '\0';

        // Update pointer and remaining space
        current_ptr += param_len + 1;
        remaining_space -= (int)(param_len + 1);

        processed_count++;
    }

    // If no parameters were processed due to insufficient space or all params being NULL,
    // and at least one non-NULL param was expected, return -1
    if (processed_count == 0 && param_count > 0) {
        // Check if there was at least one non-NULL parameter
        int has_valid_param = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < param_count; i++) {
            if (params[i] != NULL) {
                has_valid_param = 1;
                break;
            }
        }
        if (has_valid_param) {
            return -1;
        }
    }

    return processed_count;
}
